threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look puzzled when someone mentions the 'Book Club' but isn't talking about a reading group. Just wait till you see the dance floor.
Last night on the 196, a guy serenaded the whole bus with classic 90s R&B. The driver didn’t even blink. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was home? Hearing a group of friends argue passionately about which Döner is the best while sipping on a Club Mate at Herrnhuter.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you see people drinking cans of Club Mate at 8 AM while lining up for artisan bagels. It's like a morning ritual here.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know the best spots to dodge the BART delays—like popping into that hole-in-the-wall taqueria on 16th instead of waiting at Civic Center.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 49 yesterday at Civic Center only to see a dog chilling on the tracks. Muni delays are so common, I half-expected it.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists assume all Catalans are fluent in Spanish, but many in Gràcia prefer to speak Catalan, and it’s part of our identity.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they can't figure out the 'tapa' system — ordering a full dish instead of small plates is a classic rookie move.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 48 bus is reliable. Good luck with that on a Saturday night, mate.
Caught a night bus from Brixton and a bloke started singing opera. Didn't miss a beat, even the driver was tapping along. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found a pack of wild boars casually rummaging through the bins at Sonnenallee. That’s when it hit me—this place has its own wild charm.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you have Späti lounges with craft beer on tap, where you can lounge on mismatched sofas while the next-door club thumps away.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they casually side-step the dog on the tracks at Civic Center like it’s just another Tuesday commute.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Only in SF can a Muni bus stop, mid-route, to let a parade of pigeons cross the street. The driver just shook his head and smiled.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí. Really, it’s the local bars and festivals that define the soul of this place.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they still pause at the traffic lights, waiting for the green, while everyone else darts across.
